items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Engage prospects faster with Sales Agent-enriched leads
items.sum: To reach their sales goals, sales teams need to constantly generate enough pipeline. They need to decide wh…
items.desc:
To reach their sales goals, sales teams need to constantly generate enough pipeline. They need to decide which inbound leads to engage. This decision takes a lot of time because sellers must sift through large amounts of data across tools and data sources. This data includes information from internal systems like their CRM and external resources like the web and third-party data services.
Sales Agent handles the work of reviewing every available data source to create a comprehensive summary for each lead. As a seller, you can quickly decide whether to engage with a prospect or pass on them. Sales Agent reviews every lead in your queue and gathers information. It synthesizes insights that are specific to your business. At a glance, you can see if a lead is worth reaching out to and what they're interested in. Use this information to personalize your engagement with the prospect. With Sales Agent, you spend less time and get higher response rates, leading to a larger, more qualified pipeline.

items.title: SharePoint: Power Automate trigger and action for forms in SharePoint
items.sum: We are introducing new Power Automate integration capabilities for forms in SharePoint, enabling customers …
items.desc:
We are introducing new Power Automate integration capabilities for forms in SharePoint, enabling customers to create automated workflows based on responses collected using list forms, file upload forms, and document generation forms. 
With this update, a new trigger and action are now available in Power Automate:
Trigger: When a form is submitted
This operation triggers a flow when a list form, document generation form, or file upload form is submitted. Customers can use this trigger to initiate automated workflows such as approvals, notifications, or routing based on form submission events.

Action: Get form metadata

This action retrieves the metadata of a form, which includes the form ID, title, link, form type, output format, and the form questions used to collect information. This enables Power Automate flows to dynamically read form schema before executing downstream automation logic.
Disclaimer: Document generation forms are part of AI in SharePoint Public Preview. Access to DocGen Forms capabilities may be limited to customers enrolled in the preview program. List forms and file upload forms are not part of the AI in SharePoint preview.

items.desc: Microsoft is announcing the General Availability (GA) of federated Copilot connectors for Microsoft 365 Copilot. Federated Copilot connectors enable users to securely connect Copilot to popular third‑party sources and retrieve data in real time using the Model Context Protocol (MCP). These connectors do not store or index customer data in Microsoft services; access occurs in real time using the user’s identity, while administrators retain full governance and control through the Microsoft 365 admin center. items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): [Copilot Extensibility] Delete Schema Properties via Copilot Connector APIs
items.sum: We’re adding support for property deletion in custom Copilot connectors, giving admins and developers more …
items.desc:
We’re adding support for property deletion in custom Copilot connectors, giving admins and developers more control over schema evolution. With this feature, you can:
* Use Copilot connector APIs to delete or drop individual schema properties that are no longer needed.
* Keep your connector schema clean and up-to-date without requiring full re-ingestion.
* Reduce developer overhead and simplify maintenance workflows as your data models evolve.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): [Copilot Extensibility] Connector Metadata Enrichment for Custom Copilot Connectors
items.sum: We’re introducing new options that allow admins and developers to enrich their custom Copilot connectors an…
items.desc:
We’re introducing new options that allow admins and developers to enrich their custom Copilot connectors and its schema properties with descriptive metadata.
With this update, you can now: * Provide richer context about the connection and its schema properties to Copilot’s LLM, enabling more accurate and relevant responses. Add alternate names, data source categories, common actions to your connector and description for schema properties. * Improve search and chat quality by helping Copilot better understand the semantics and purpose of your data.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): [Copilot Extensibility] Simplified People Resolution for Custom Copilot Connectors
items.sum: This feature enables connectors to resolve external identities—such as email addresses or user IDs from thi…
items.desc:
This feature enables connectors to resolve external identities—such as email addresses or user IDs from third-party data sources—to their corresponding Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) identities. By allowing flexible identity mapping, developers can:
* Simplify integration workflows without needing complex Graph API lookups.
* Reduce setup friction by avoiding strict dependency on object IDs.
* Improve search and chat relevance in Copilot by ensuring accurate people attribution across connected systems.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): [Copilot Extensibility] Schema Recommendations for Custom Connectors in M365 Admin Center
items.sum: Accelerate your custom connector development with intelligent schema guidance directly in the Microsoft 365…
items.desc:
Accelerate your custom connector development with intelligent schema guidance directly in the Microsoft 365 Admin Center. This feature provides:
* Proposed schema suggestions tailored to the connector you’re building.
* Real-time recommendations to identify gaps or errors in your schema design to help improve search and chat results in Copilot.
Whether you're starting from scratch or refining an existing connector, this enhancement ensures your schema is optimized for Copilot extensibility and performance.

Users will be able to now view Adoption metrics on either a monthly or weekly timeframe for specific groups of interest, as well as deep dive into Impact metrics like Copilot Assisted Hours, Actions Taken, and feature-specific dimensions.

items.desc: When a call is forwarded/transferred to another colleague, users can prompt Copilot to automatically summarize the context, open questions, etc. for the call as a briefing for the receiving colleague. This feature ensures that the recipient is well-informed about the previous conversations related to the transferred call, enhancing their understanding and preparedness.

items.sum: Copilot in Teams will now have a 'stop' button after sending a prompt. This will allow the user to stop Cop…
items.desc: Copilot in Teams will now have a 'stop' button after sending a prompt. This will allow the user to stop Copilot's response either before the response has started to generate, or even after the response is generating. The user may then start a new prompt if they wish.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Copilot in Teams meetings responses can be exported to Word and Excel
items.sum: Now, certain responses from Copilot in Teams meetings will have an option to ‘edit in’ Word or Excel. The ‘…
items.desc: Now, certain responses from Copilot in Teams meetings will have an option to ‘edit in’ Word or Excel. The ‘edit in’ option for Word will be available for Copilot responses that are long (more than 1300 characters) and will automatically open a Word document with the response written. The ‘edit in’ option for Excel will be available for Copilot responses that are formatted in a table, and will automatically open an Excel file with the formatted response added. Copilot responses can only be exported if the meeting policy for the meeting is set to allow copying or forwarding of meeting chat, live captions, transcript, and Copilot responses. The export capability will also inherit the sensitivity label of the meeting (sensitivity labels are available to Teams Premium users).
